steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. The question asks what New Caledonian crows are “well known for” according to the text. The answer must come directly from the passage, not from outside knowledge.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘They have larger brains than palm cockatoos have.’: the passage never compares brain sizes between any animals. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘They live longer than octopuses do.’: the passage never mentions lifespans of any animal. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘They create tools out of twigs.’: the passage states directly that “New Caledonian crows are well known for creating hooks and spears from twigs.” ✓
  • Choice D — ‘They use leaves as tools.’: the passage attributes leaf use to palm cockatoos, not New Caledonian crows. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
